What it tests: Whether imaging and molecular data are linked one patient to one patient
Required evidence: Attrition report linkage manifest duplicate check
Minimum pass criterion: Explicit inner-join count with no duplicate expansion
If the gate fails: Any downstream performance result is uninterpretable
Practitioner action: Stop and repair linkage before modeling
LEMPA anchor: V1 paired set reached 130 unique patients with no duplicate expansion

What it tests: Whether the chosen deconvolution or prediction branch can run without collapse or pathological output
Required evidence: Smoke test QC summary non-degeneracy checks
If the gate fails: The method branch is not usable for evidence generation
Practitioner action: Archive or replace that branch; do not claim method failure was only biological
LEMPA anchor: V2-G1 passed for the scaden branch

What it tests: Whether the target surface is stable across at least two plausible deconvolution methods
Minimum pass criterion: Predeclared agreement threshold met at the intended resolution
If the gate fails: Target instability appears before prediction and invalidates strong downstream claims
Practitioner action: Collapse targets only under predeclared rules or stop the line
LEMPA anchor: V2-G2 failed with top-10 median -0.087 and 1/10 at r >= 0.4

What it tests: Whether downstream modeling uses a fixed target table rather than a moving target definition
Required evidence: Timestamped abundance or target freeze file used downstream
Minimum pass criterion: Frozen target table exists before B3/B4 evaluation
If the gate fails: Downstream estimates are vulnerable to silent analytic drift
Practitioner action: Re-freeze the target set and rerun downstream steps transparently

What it tests: Whether imaging recovers the target surface at the declared endpoint
Required evidence: Cross-validated metric table and threshold rule
If the gate fails: The line remains a negative benchmark not a validated predictor
Practitioner action: Report the negative result directly; do not soften failure into trend language
LEMPA anchor: V1 failed the >=10 cell-type rule; V2-G4 failed with 1/4 modules passing

What it tests: Whether performance survives site batch histology or external-cohort separation
Required evidence: Feasibility memo or held-out/external performance table
Minimum pass criterion: Robustness surface is estimable and passes declared criteria
If the gate fails: Internal findings cannot be generalized or transported confidently
Practitioner action: Defer validation claims and seek trigger-grade external resources
LEMPA anchor: V2-G5 was skipped because frozen strata were insufficient

What it tests: Whether any residual positive-looking association is reproducible and independent of leakage
Required evidence: Hypothesis table recomputation target-construction audit join-integrity audit
Minimum pass criterion: Recomputed values match frozen results and target independence is demonstrated
If the gate fails: Residual associations should be suppressed or heavily caveated
Practitioner action: Keep only subordinate audited remainders; do not let them rescue a failed line
